identitySentence: The Kuzmich Law Firm PC is a Flower Mound personal injury firm where founding partner Steve Kuzmich, who has practiced law since 1994, helps injured people and families across Denton County.

concept.headline: Injured in Denton County? Talk to The Kuzmich Law Firm Before You Talk to the Insurance Company.
concept.subheadline: After a car wreck, a fall, or a serious injury, you should not have to wonder whether you have a case or what it costs to find out. Tell us what happened: consultations are free, and you will hear back with clear next steps and real appointment times.
credibilityPoints: Founding partner Steve Kuzmich has practiced law since 1994 and is Board Certified in Personal Injury Trial Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization
Attorney Sean J. Kilgore served as a prosecutor in the Denton County Criminal District Attorney's Office
Attorney Nicole Gordillo is bilingual (se habla espanol) and has been with the firm since 2000
Serving clients statewide from the firm's Flower Mound office in Denton County

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The Kuzmich Law Firm, P.C. practices personal injury law and nothing else. The firm handles car, truck, motorcycle, and bus accidents, wrongful death claims, dog bites, slip and fall and other premises liability cases, child injuries, burn injuries, and catastrophic and spinal cord injuries, along with product-injury claims involving defective truck brakes, baby formula, and GLP-1 products.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The firm serves clients statewide from its office at 4315 Windsor Centre Trail in Flower Mound, in the heart of Denton County, and its attorneys are members of the Denton County Bar Association. Sean J. Kilgore built part of his career inside the Denton County Criminal District Attorney's Office.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your consultation is free and carries no obligation. The firm will ask what happened, when and where it happened, what injuries you suffered, and what treatment you have received, and you will get an honest assessment of whether you have a case worth pursuing.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To The Kuzmich Law Firm, P.C.,

Steve Kuzmich began practicing law in 1994, and for three decades your firm has represented injured people from Denton County, today from your Flower Mound office, with a team that includes a former Denton County prosecutor and a bilingual attorney who has been with the firm since 2000.

We build one thing: a Lead Capturer, a custom contact form and first-response system designed for a single firm. Yours is already built, and we would like you to test it.

Why it fits your practice: personal injury inquiries rarely arrive at a tidy moment. They come after wrecks, after diagnoses, late at night, and the person writing is often contacting several firms the same evening. Your website already does the hard work: clear practice-area pages, published case results, your board-certification record, and a free consultation form backed by a 24-hour answering service. What it cannot do is answer back. There is no online scheduling on the site, so every form submission waits for a person before the potential client hears anything. Your Lead Capturer closes that gap the moment an inquiry arrives.

Here is what it does:

1. Every inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox, with the likely matter type identified in the subject line.
2. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to what they wrote: a car accident inquiry gets a car accident response, not a generic acknowledgment.
3. That response presents your genuinely available consultation times.
4. A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the consultations on your calendar are committed ones.

Every response is produced by fixed rules and text your firm reviews and approves before anything goes live. Nothing improvises in your name.

Your Lead Capturer opens with an identity sentence written specifically for your firm:

"The Kuzmich Law Firm PC is a Flower Mound personal injury firm where founding partner Steve Kuzmich, who has practiced law since 1994, helps injured people and families across Denton County."

We would rather prove this than pitch it. For 15 days you can test the complete experience end to end: submit inquiries, watch the responses, reserve an appointment in a payment sandbox, all before you decide anything. No revenue projections, no performance promises: just the system working, for you to judge.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information anywhere in the test.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email, an actual message and not a dashboard entry.
4. Confirm the notification contains every field the form displayed.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email, so your staff replies to the person directly.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to your TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email with no action from your staff.
9. Confirm the customer address actually receives it, checking the spam folder too.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual topic of the inquiry; it should read as if written for that message, because it was.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears before the appointment is confirmed.
14. Confirm the preparation list the client receives fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In the payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event, never before.

If any step fails, the test fails: contact us and we will make it right or withdraw the offer.

If it passes, we would welcome a short conversation about putting your Lead Capturer in front of real inquiries.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
